user_message: This just came in from Angela Doyle at Contoso Ltd. Can you prep a brief?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-09 09:48 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Contoso Ltd renewal risk

Just checking in on the renewal risk proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
Date: 2024-01-06 15:09 EST
Subject: Re: renewal risk Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the renewal risk proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: renewal risk Discussion - Contoso Ltd
Date: 2024-01-10
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), David (Contoso Ltd)

[00:02:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on renewal risk from our last session.

[00:08:00] David (Contoso Ltd): On the renewal risk front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:10:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): On the renewal risk front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:12:00] David (Contoso Ltd): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] David (Contoso Ltd): On the renewal risk front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:19:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-L trap (Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-L failure mode (Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-L -- Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
